posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.content:
WHAT IS IT?

In short, it works similar to the Tapology Survivor game in the sense that it continues every week until there's a winner (or in some cases in this game no winner) but instead of picking your fighters, get assigned a random fighter from the upcoming UFC card and hope they win.

FORMAT:

1) To enter, simply say that you want to enter at the start of a new game. The length of the games depend entirely on how long it takes to either find a winner or eliminate everyone.

2) Then, you will randomly be assigned a fighter from the upcoming UFC card. This will be decided by me using this site I found wheeldecide.com and putting all the fighters names on a wheel and going through the list of players one by one and spinning the wheel to decide which fighter they get. I'll make a very low quality video of the draw and post it here to show I'm not deliberately messing with anyone in particular. It might mean that some people end up with the same fighter but that doesn't affect the game as it's more of an everybody fighting for themselves kinda game rather than a head to head game.

3) If your randomly assigned fighter wins their fight then you're through to the next round. If their fight get cancelled or postponed then I'll count it as a loss as it would be considered unlucky. Draws and no contests will also count as losses for the same reason. This happens every week until there is only one left and they will be the winner. In a unique twist to make the game as stupid as possible, if everyone eventually goes out, then nobody wins the game and the game will be marked in the record books as "nobody ******* won" or something like that. For example, if there are 2 or 3 people left and they all have losing fighters then nobody wins the game. I feel this also adds weight to the game as you have to be REALLY lucky to be a winner as you literally have to be luckier than every other competitor to be the only one left standing!

WHAT DOES THE WINNER GET?

A place on the list of winners that will be displayed on the opening post of every new game and their current AV put on the new game poster (see below) in the middle of the horseshow so they effectively become the poster boy/girl/other for the new game!

Any questions, just ask! And remember, please don't take this game too seriously! I mean, it's practically a satire of the Survivor Game when you think about it lol!

For the sake of record keeping and historical intrigue, here's the link to the Tapology Lucky Bastard Game: 1st Edition that was on a separate thread: https://www.tapology.com/forum/threads/68098

posts.author: Steve Terry
posts.content:
In light of the claims made by @Troz , @Cozstradamus , @qqqqqq , @ITSYAABOYSB and several other American Tapology users of honourable standing regarding an alleged "Anti-American bias" from @The Wheel , I have decided to launch and official inquiry into these accusations. The inquiry will be led by me and features a panel of some of the finest individuals with strong links to both the United Kingdom and The United States of America.

To my far right, we have Brad Guzan. Born in Evergreen Park, Illinois, he is a former professional football (soccer) goalkeeper who made 64 appearances for US national team, appearing at two FIFA World Cups and also represented them at the 2008 Summer Olympics. He has also gained a cult hero status over in the UK for his stint playing in England, most notably for Aston Villa, making 144 appearances for them between 2008 and 2016.

To my immediate right, we have Ogugua "OG" Anunoby. Born in London, England, he is a basketball player who plays as a small forward/power forward for the New York Knicks in the NBA. He won the 2019 NBA Championship with the Toronto Raptors and was also named in the NBA All-Defensive Team in 2023 after leading the league in steals. He is widely considered to be one of, if not the best British player in NBA history.

To my immediate left, we have Mischa Barton. Born in London, England, she is a British and American actress whose career has spanned stage, film and TV. She has most notably starred in major films such as "Notting Hill" and "The Sixth Sense" as well as the TV series "The O.C." along with many other independent projects. She has also ventured into the world of fashion design and has been an ambassador for many charitable organisations such as "Climate Star" as well raising money for women's cancer research.

Finally, but by no means least, on my far left we have The Right Honourable Sir Robert Liston (not to be confused with the Scottish surgeon of the same name). Born in Kirkliston, Scotland, he was a British politician who served as the ambassador for many countries, including holding the office of "Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to the United States" which was the precursor to the modern role of the "British Ambassador To The United States", a position currently held by Sir Christian Turner. He was also said to have been able to speak 10 different languages which is no mean feat in a world before Duolingo.

I have no doubt that this panel of extraordinary individuals will come to a fair and informed conclusion regarding this matter. We openly invite anyone of any nationality to put forward their evidence and arguments for the panel to consider, although we are especially keen to hear from Americans who feel they have been slighted by @The Wheel , not due to natural misfortune as a result of the nature of this game but because of their nationality.
